title: Remember It! The Names of People You Meet, All of Your Passwords, Where You Left Your Keys, and Everything Else You Tend to Forget
subtitle: A practical guide to improving everyday memory using ancient techniques adapted for modern life.
author: Nelson Dellis
themes: Memory is a learnable skill, not a fixed capacity
Visualization and association are the core of all memory techniques
The memory palace (method of loci) for structured recall
Name-face memorization as a practical daily skill
Chunking and the PAO (Person-Action-Object) system for numbers
awards: Featured on The Today Show, CNN, NPR
excerpt: Four-time USA Memory Champion Nelson Dellis shows you how to remember the things you forget every day — names, passwords, where you left your keys, and more. Using ancient techniques adapted for the modern world, Dellis provides a step-by-step system that anyone can learn, regardless of age or natural memory ability.
